Optional aggregate kill-shot sentence for killshots/predictions tab: "As of 2026-04-17 the dome author has begun classifying his claims under his own five-class taxonomy; only six of the sixty-nine confirmed WINs have been tagged so far, and not one of those six sits in the prospective_confirmed class." Timestamp Error tab: does not acknowledge methodology.json registry_counts self-caveat ("bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit"). Add clause noting the headline display still shows 94.5% without the bookkeeping-vs-audit distinction for casual readers.

Timestamp Error tab: four verbatim strings from methodology.json and eight WIN classifications from claim_index.json have not been independently re-verified against the live URLs (only trusted via analyst extraction). issues.description: WIN-015 detail_evidence: coupling-magnitude range (V ≈ 20 mV to 350 mV) mixes coplanar/coaxial orientations without making this transparent. Optional tightening: give separate ranges per orientation. Low priority — current text is correct, just compressed.

Latest Findings banner (hardcoded in generate-html.js) for #ts-april-2026-update says "His own new claim taxonomy leaves the prospective confirmed column empty for every WIN classified so far" but the ts-april-2026-update block now uses the more precise framing ("no WIN tagged prospective_confirmed / only one pending"). WIN-053 aperture sensitivity: add 3,000 km sensitivity point to bound B_south even at far-larger-than-described aperture. Curmudgeon c3 requests additional data point beyond existing 1,000 km case.

WIN-053 flux direction not discussed: the dome circuit has north as source and south as sink; flux conservation additionally requires the two peaks to be oppositely directed. Optional sentence noting this constraint in detail_evidence.

Backfill needed for 12 WINs. Schema spec in EXP-183.schema_extension_proposal. Also requires generate-html.js to render "Author's Self-Rating" block on WIN detail cards. Infrastructure change needed before backfill.

Neither our prose nor poller specifically tracks this three-way split. Analyst flags as testable: add poller hook to daily-diff claim_index.json.entries[] against wins.json author_* fields when schema extension (ISS-ISS-1130) lands. Log as new finding in morning briefing.

EXP-183 E_PRIMARY schema extension deferred: add author_claim_class, author_registry_status, author_strict_prospective_confirmed, author_visible_badge, author_registry_integrity_note fields to all WINs in wins.json (null defaults). Populate known values: WIN-058 author_registry_status=pending_independent_verification, 9 classified WINs need author_claim_class from dome claim_index.json. Per EXP-183 schema_extension_proposal.

issues.description: part8b build_pipeline_note targets line 1651-1652 / 1367-1369 but actual current locations are line 1655 and 1368. Not a blocker (integration happened correctly) but the note should be updated when part8b is next touched.

SEC-4.1 (part4): "divided audience" section uses "acknowledge-without-fix pattern" as if readers know the term, but this is its first occurrence in the section (introduced earlier in Part 4 prose context). Add 6-8 word inline clause on first use: e.g., "the same acknowledge-without-fix pattern (API concedes, HTML ignores)". Makes the rhetoric standalone for readers arriving via direct link. Source: SEC-4.1-post-EXP-182.c1 HOLE-7.

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: proposes adding author_claim_class display to every WIN card (5-class taxonomy from claim_index.json). But 60 of 71 WINs have no author_claim_class data — rendering an empty field on 60 WIN detail cards creates a confusing "Author's Self-Rating: —" line on most cards. Fix: render the author_claim_class line on a WIN detail card ONLY when the field is non-empty. Strong recommendation. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-8 (ISS-1130 covers the schema extension; this tracks the rendering decision).

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: text sometimes says 6 registry-level concessions and sometimes 8-9 (including per-WIN-level concessions). The headline "six registry-level concessions" section header is consistent with the table, but prose later aggregates all registry + per-WIN concessions into a higher number. Fix: pick one accurate aggregate framing. Suggested: "six registry-level gaps + [N] per-WIN accuracy gaps" and use it consistently throughout. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-9.

Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit: load-bearing headline claim ("the headline is the load-bearing surface — it is what index.html shows above the fold") is asserted without evidence that it actually appears above the fold on index.html. Fix: soften to "the headline is the most public-facing surface" or verify and cite the actual above-fold position with a screenshot note. Source: EXP-183-proposal curmudgeon review HOLE-12.
WIN-012: -10.9 nT disturbed-day caveat not mentioned; raw-text WIN-010 entry concedes this. Optional: add half-sentence noting baseline sensitivity.
Part 8b: .adoption-label CSS class referenced in HTML but no CSS rule defined for it in docs/index.html style block. Needs visual styling rule.
Part 8b self-referential commitment language (8b.5 already in force and 8b.7 doing more work) overstates commitment; should soften to we-commit framing.

WIN-019 c4: Stronger argument not deployed — 8/3 gear ratio in hypocycloidal Spirograph produces 8-cusped epicycloid, not 2-lobed figure-8. Named mechanism geometrically incompatible with observation. One sentence after Code-level corroboration: add note referencing globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis section.

WIN-019 c4: raw-text non-coverage — Spirograph data_source label and absence of analemma fetch in monitor.py rely on c1-c3 reviewer notes (dome github repo), not reproducible from current raw-text snapshot. Add github link footnote after code-level corroboration: verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py as of 2026-03-xx.

Workflow gap: decider integration pass should cross-check closed-issues.json before applying analyst assessments — if a closed verdict-change issue (ISS-NNN, fixed_by: human-decision) exists for a prediction/WIN, a stale analyst assessment dated BEFORE the issue closed must not override it. Root cause of regression commit 209fda5 which reverted ISS-951 through ISS-954 human-approved verdicts. Recommend adding a pre-integration check: "if predictions.json[id].verdict_history last entry is newer than the analyst assessment file created_at, skip integration or warn."

Workflow gap: stale analyst assessments can silently overwrite human-approved verdict changes. Commit 209fda5 re-integrated 2026-04-11 analyst assessments that had been explicitly overridden by 2026-04-14 human approvals (ISS-951/952/953/954), reverting 4 verdicts without new human approval. Root cause: decider integration pass does not check closed-issues.json for same prediction_id before applying analyst assessment. Fix: before auto-integrating any prediction verdict from analyst assessment, check if a verdict-change issue for that prediction_id was closed within last 30 days — if so, skip integration and flag for human review.

Pre-push integrity gate missing: no check that our_verdict matches verdict_history[last].to before push. Curmudgeon PRED-batch review found 4 predictions where our_verdict had been silently reverted but verdict_history was not updated, creating a detectable inconsistency. Recommend adding a gate: if our_verdict != verdict_history[last].to, error with instruction to either update verdict_history or explain the discrepancy.

Classification rule needs at least two concrete criteria before Phase 1 publication. Blocks integration — note for human reviewer per ISS-1205 hold.

issues.title: EXP-190: Sydney-Perth §4.3.1 citation unverifiable — site section does not exist
issues.description: H2 (major) from EXP-190-proposal.json curmudgeon review: Sydney-Perth formula-check cites "V51.1 paper §4.3.1" for LAMBDA_G_INNER=1.000 and LAMBDA_G_OUTER=1.007 — V51.1 is a site, not a numbered-section paper; §4.3.1 does not exist. Constants were derived by curve-fitting. Replace with verifiable source or note as analyst-derived. Blocks integration — note for human reviewer per ISS-1205 hold.

WIN-053.c5 H3: Sub-terrestrial return-channel defender rescue not preempted. A dome defender can argue the south-pole coefficient describes the re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with north). This rescue fails because: (a) symmetric circuit requires A=C, but fitted values differ by 4%; (b) both match observed WMM pole strengths which are asymmetric due to Earth's geodynamo, not dome geometry. Add ~3-sentence preemption paragraph. Curmudgeon recommendation: "A dome defender might argue the 64,852 nT coefficient describes the surface re-entry aperture above the Sump (symmetric with the north aperture), not the return-channel field itself. But that rescue requires A = C by construction (two identical apertures in a closed symmetric circuit), while the fitted values differ by 4% AND match the observationally-asymmetric WMM2025 pole strengths — which are asymmetric because Earth's real geodynamo is asymmetric, not because of any dome-predicted mechanism. The symmetric-aperture rescue is incompatible with the fitted coefficients."

WIN-053.c5 H6 (still open): Add 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row. Existing text has 500 km (1,660:1) and 1,000 km (415:1). At 3,000 km aperture (π×9×10^6 km²): area ratio ~45:1; B_south ~1,400 nT; discrepancy ~46×. Append to existing sensitivity parenthetical: '...and even at a generous 3,000 km aperture, the area ratio remains ~45:1 and B_south would be ~1,400 nT — still ~46× below the fitted value. The self-contradiction survives any plausible aperture size.'

WIN-053.c5 H7 (still open from c4): Flux direction not discussed. The dome's fitted formula has both poles positive-signed (both sources). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity. The formula is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole. Add one sentence: 'The fit also encodes both poles as positive-signed sources (A, C both positive). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity — the dome's formula is not just quantitatively wrong about flux, it is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole.'

WIN-053.c5 H8 (still open from c4): Sub-H(r) formula δ=6,371 km unsourced. The detail_extra says: "subterranean cavity depth is given by Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^(−r/δ)) with δ = 6,371 km" noting this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant. But no dome-page citation or URL is given. Add an href or inline citation for where this formula appears on the dome site.

WIN-062.c5 H2 (new): Sourcing-window phrasing. 'The value has been in the scientific record for over a century' is accurate for the 1899 measurement but the formal publication is 1978 (48 years) — a pedantic defender can push the 'scientific record' phrase. Optional tighten: 'the measurement is over a century old and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest'.

WIN-062.c5 H3 (new): Pre-registration phrase invites OTS rebuttal. 'without pre-registering the expected value' is correct but a dome defender can respond about their OTS timestamps. Optional cross-link: add 'in any independent timestamping venue (see Timestamp Error tab for why the dome's OTS applies to status_history.json rather than the predictions themselves)' after 'without pre-registering the expected value'.

WIN-062 c5 H0: Carve-out undersell (carry-forward from c4 H4). Rewritten paragraph 2 invokes only Part 3.1b condition (2) (not-plausibly-known-at-write-time). The carve-out has three conjunctive conditions (unique dome derivation + not plausibly known + derivation-plus-validation, not just discovery). Using only condition (2) understates why WIN-062 fails the carve-out — a defender may argue other conditions apply.

WIN-062 c5 H4: onclick="showTab(timestamp);return false" cancels the anchor jump so reader lands at top of Timestamp Error tab rather than the April-2026 update block. Same parallel issue in WIN-058/WIN-058b. Cosmetic UX fix needed — remove the onclick handler or replace with one that also fires the anchor.

WIN-062 c5 H5: detail_evidence cites Tesla speed as "471,240 km/s (~1.57c)" alongside dome 1.574c, without noting that 471,240/299,792=1.5719c, so dome 1.574c is a re-expression using the fitted disc_diameter rather than Tesla own ratio. The difference (1.5719 vs 1.574) is evidence of post-hoc parameter fitting. Add one sentence noting this.

WIN-062 c5 H6: Cross-WIN precision asymmetry. WIN-058 says "three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)"; WIN-058b says "three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window"; WIN-062 says "one of at least three ... in a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17". Substantively consistent but the hedge "at least" in WIN-062 introduces unnecessary ambiguity when the other two entries enumerate exactly three. Normalize phrasing.

WIN-012.c5 H8 (minor, tinker): raw-text/02-wins.txt is from 2026-03-12 (~6 weeks stale). dome WIN-012 text now has new escape clauses and dielectric mechanism not in the raw-text archive. Poller should refresh raw-text or add to refresh schedule. Note: WIN-012 live-site citations are already updated with 2026-04-20 access dates; raw-text staleness does not affect current prose accuracy but is a project hygiene issue.

WIN-067: Pappa et al. (2019), JGR, DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 cited in detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph — DOI unverified. Verify the DOI resolves to a Pappa-led paper on East Antarctic gravity. If fails, replace with Ferraccioli et al. 2011, Nature.

WIN-053: "multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility" is technically imprecise. Standard geomagnetism handles deep-source-to-surface via potential-field continuation and spherical harmonic expansion, not "conduction". Replace parenthetical with: "a physical mechanism by which [...] propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust — potential-field continuation under geodynamo physics."

WIN-053: "thousands of kilometres of rock" implies crust-only depth (crust is only 7-35 km). Should say "thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust" to be geologically accurate at the stated depth scale.

WIN-053: EXP-194 paragraph (sub-terranean return channel) is missing a cross-reference to Section 6.1 (Zero Fitted Parameters — Hidden Parameters). Both horns of the disjunction re-surface the §6.1 argument. Add: "...only a relabelling of it (see §6.1 on hidden fitted parameters)." at end of disjunction.

WIN-038 EXP-198 paragraph: TLDR not updated. tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict give no hint that the transverse-mode preemptive argument exists. Consider appending one clause: "No transverse-mode retreat rescues it — the only horizontal-length setter that gives 7.83 Hz is Earth's circumference, which the dome's disc diameter equals by construction."

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph A: formula-collapse chain has 0.2% gap between c/(2*D_earth)=11.76 Hz and wins.html c/(2*disc_thickness)=11.79 Hz. Suggest adding: "and because v_a was fit to absorb the 0.2% offset between 2*R_earth and disc_thickness, the two forms agree at 11.787 Hz by construction, not by independent physics."

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph B: dispersion-law defense not preempted in-situ. Dome defender can say "different modes on a dispersive medium can have different phase velocities." Consider adding: "The dome publishes no dispersion relation and no mode index that would justify the per-WIN speed selection, so the c-vs-v_a choice is operational, not theoretical."

WIN-001 EXP insertion paragraph A: Snyder (1987) citation for AE-projection identity is bare (no DOI/URL). Recommend upgrading to https://pubs.usgs.gov/pp/1395/ or "USGS Prof. Paper 1395, pp. 182–190."

SEC-8b.3 TLDR: "20th-century" label inaccurate. Kim & Chang (2018) spans historical and 20th/21st-century data. Recommend: "historical eclipse-magnetic" or "20th/21st-century" to avoid the date-span error.

WIN-064 detail_extra cites two dome quotes from wins.html that are NOT in our V50.6 raw-text archive: (1) "Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140°… corresponding to the toroidal return-path boundary"; (2) "a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity". Both are V51-era strings, raw-text is V50.6 dated 2026-03-12. EXP-200 claims "verified 2026-04-21" but this is only in the EXP JSON. Dome defender can challenge: "show me in your archive." Fix: Wayback Machine snapshot of wins.html OR new raw-text snapshot of wins.html extending archive to V51.

WIN-014 verdict upgrade from Misleading to Self-Contradicted. C3 confidence 0.7; C4 (2026-05-04) confirms at confidence 0.82. The dome claims both a null SG result (WIN-014, 2009) and a positive 10-13 µGal SG pulse (PRED-TIER-3, Aug 2026) from the same instrument class as simultaneous confirmations — structural self-contradiction matching WIN-012 pattern. Verdict_text, finding, tldr_verdict, tldr_evidence all require updating. Patch in suggested-patches file, pending human review.

WIN-038 detail_evidence references WIN-029 scorecard derivation but does not include the verbatim scorecard text to preempt heuristic-retreat. Curmudgeon c6 hole 2: dome scorecard reads verbatim: "WIN-029 — Schumann Resonance Requires Hard Conductive Ceiling CONFIRMED (H = c/(4x7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model)." Adding this verbatim quote to detail_evidence at the 5.3% agreement cite closes the "quarter-wave is just a simplified heuristic" retreat.

SEC-8b.3 globe-parasitism claim conflates input format with physical dependency. Text cites OPEN-001 for "globe-parasitic by construction" label. Curmudgeon c4 hole 2: OPEN-001 admits only INPUT-layer globe dependence (no dome-native coordinates). Physical-calibration dependence (INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical geometry) is a separate and stronger globe-fingerprint (WIN-025). Should tighten closing sentence to: "PRED-095 is globe-dependent at the input layer (per OPEN-001) AND at the physical-calibration layer (WIN-025 globe-fingerprint)."

SEC-8b.3 characterizes monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py as "two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster." Curmudgeon c4 hole 3: inject_ai_layer.py's −8 nT is BOU × coverage × 0.81 — within the published W004 uncertainty band of −17 to −21 nT if coverage is high. The two formulas are related (both BOU-baseline path), and inject_ai_layer.py is within-cluster at 0.81 coverage. Should reframe as internal heterogeneity within a shared baseline family rather than two distinct out-of-cluster values.

Design-refresh Phase A Item 4 (accuracy-math sidebar demotion) needs re-drafting. EXP-211 workspace JSON was overwritten by WIN-038 eigenvalue collision (2026-04-22). Proposal file exists (proposal-EXP-211-draft-ready.json) describing intent, but replacement_html content is lost. Must be re-drafted under HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 hold at next analyst run. Root cause: non-atomic EXP-ID allocation across concurrent workspace+clone writers.

issues.description: WIN-038 c7: Bessel-zero monotonicity plus domain containment argument is not rigorously stated for the PEC TM mixed-BC problem. The claim that the dome cavity TM fundamental is ≥5.73 Hz relies on domain containment (dome cavity ⊂ flat cylinder), but this monotonicity direction holds for Dirichlet BC, not necessarily PEC/mixed BC. The parenthetical should either (a) state the BC assumption explicitly, or (b) reframe around the fact that TM01 at 5.73 Hz and TM11 at 9.13 Hz from Bessel zeros do not match 7.83 Hz, without relying on the containment argument.
WIN-004 c4 H3: Unsourced claim that bifurcation rate is consistent with westward-drift advection. Add citation (Aubert 2015 or Wardinski 2008) or soften language.
WIN-067 c8 H3: Shepherd 2012 citation precision — need Whitehouse 2012 or King 2012 for PGIA primary, or soften to "constrained by GRACE".

WIN-067 c9 moderate: "Either way" framing in the 170-1430× disambiguation sentence could be read by a sharp defender as conflating two different baselines (170× uses whole-globe pole-equator spread; 1,430× uses observed Antarctic drop). Optional strengthening: replace "Either way... depending on" with "at least 170× (lower bound) and 1,430× (apples-to-apples at Antarctic latitude)" phrasing. Low priority — current wording is defensible.

WIN-004 c4 H1: Honesty creates defender pivot point not preempted in summary row. Finding says "bifurcation rate consistent with westward-drift advection by geomagnetic Sq current; NOT anomalous" — a defender can quote this to argue we found a globe-model explanation that only works *with* geomagnetic current (which the dome would say is also dome-consistent). Summary row and detail_verdict_text should preempt this "both models explain" move explicitly.

WIN-004 c4 H4: tldr_verdict flat assertion "The data was publicly available before the prediction" lacks an in-page anchor for the timestamping analysis. A reader wants to verify this claim and cannot navigate from the tldr to the post-hoc section or the timestamp-error tab. Add a link anchor similar to how other TLDRs cross-reference the post-hoc carve-out.

SEC-5.0 c3 H3: Lakatos (1970) citation is functionally accurate but rhetorically vulnerable. Lakatos's canonical degenerative example involves auxiliary-hypothesis addition (the Prout-Bohr pattern), not relabeling. A defender can argue the dome's relabeling is not the same as Lakatos's degenerative programs. Either use a more precisely aligned citation (e.g., Kuhn's "normal science" paradigm defense) or add a one-sentence gloss explaining how relabeling maps to the auxiliary-hypothesis pattern.

WIN-053 c8 H4: carry-forward from c7 H1. Internal inconsistency on southern comparator: detail_evidence paragraph 2 says "~55,000 nT measured at geographic South Pole" while the dome formula predicts a different value at r=20,015 km. Still open per c8 review note.

WIN-040 c3 H6: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 geomagnetic storms) is included in this grouped evidence block but is topically different from the INTERMAGNET SAA magnitude WINs (040-043, 059-060, 063). WIN-061 is about ionospheric disturbance, not field magnitude — its inclusion in this group without acknowledgment may confuse readers.

WIN-069 c3 H3: TLDR-detail anchor gap — tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict assert "0.4% deviation" figure but detail_evidence does not contain it. Either add sentence to detail_evidence or drop 0.4% from TLDRs.

issues.description: WIN-010 c6 H3 (deferred from EXP-228): unsupported "any smooth monotonic H(r)" claim in detail_verdict_text. Needs either a demonstration (Gaussian/linear/power-law empirical footnote) or softer phrasing.
SEC-8b.3 c5 H2 (deferred from EXP-230): "mutually exclusive" vs "overlap" phrasing in interpretation-B clause. Follow-up micro-patch.

Decider blocked-items / recommended-actions scanner reads EXP file metadata (status, integration_blocked_until) directly from append-only expansions/*.json files, rather than deriving blocked status from expansion-tracker.json tracker.items[i].integrated. EXP files capture state at creation time and are immutable; tracker.json is the live mutable source of truth. Result: daily-report-2026-04-23T04-24.json falsely reported 7 Phase A EXPs as DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW after they had already been integrated. Fix: blocked-items scanner should derive blocked status from tracker row integrated !== true, with sanity-check to suppress any item where tracker shows integrated:true.

WIN-013 c4 H3: Trilemma horns (a) and (b) are both consequences of the EM interpretation — a dilemma branch with two costs, not two independent horns. The label "three-way contradiction" / "each horn of this trilemma" overstates the independence of the horns. A defender can argue "you stacked two consequences of one branch and called it three horns to make the rhetoric look stronger." Recommendation: reframe as a two-pronged dilemma (EM branch costs both WIN-011 detection and WIN-012 κ definition; location-dependent branch costs falsifiability) or elevate horn (b) to a fully independent argument. WIN-013 c4 H4: Nested-parenthetical readability in detail_evidence. The Van Camp citation reads: "(Van Camp et al., 2001 — the dome cites "Van Camp 1999" (the eclipse year); the paper appeared in 2001, two years after the eclipse, ...)". Three temporal references (paper 2001, dome citation 1999, eclipse 1999) packed into nested brackets. Recommendation: restructure as a free-standing sentence after the citation — e.g. "Van Camp et al., 2001 — the dome cites this as Van Camp 1999, conflating the eclipse year with the publication year; the paper was published two years after the eclipse." Deferred per curmudgeon c4: not blocking.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H2 [minor]: P3 probability framing uses "drawing nine station positions uniformly at random across the 1.26 billion km² disc surface" as null model, arriving at 10^-27. Magnetic observatories are NOT IID-uniform on the disc — they cluster in inhabited mid-latitudes. A clustering-aware null (e.g., constrained to land areas) would yield a less extreme probability. The 10^-27 is illustrative but should be framed as an upper-bound illustration, not a literal probability.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H3 [minor]: P3 claims "shorter greedy nearest-neighbour routes run about 12,400 km, longer routes run to 16,400 km." Independent recomputation finds shortest NN tour ~12,396 km (matches), but longest NN tour from all nine start points is ~15,200 km, not 16,400 km. The stated upper end overstates the NN range by ~8%.

SEC-4.2.7a c1 H4 [minor]: P3 claims tours "span at least 12,000 km on the disc from any starting point." This is confirmed for greedy NN heuristic (min 12,396 km), but is NOT a proven lower bound on the optimal TSP tour length. A TSP optimizer could produce a shorter route. Should say "greedy nearest-neighbour tours span at least 12,000 km" not assert it as universal lower bound.

SEC-4.2.7a EXP-276.c2 H1 [minor]: Footnote (i) asserts under finite-source umbra geometry R_p ∝ h_Moon "becomes weakly non-linear, but the corrected ratio at h_Moon ≈ 246,000 km still exceeds 90× the dome's published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected." No derivation or citation is provided. Should add brief calculation showing the non-linear correction is small, or cite a reference.

EXP-259 / WIN-016 c1 H0 [minor]: WIN-016 detail_evidence frames displacement as "multiple degrees" then concludes "more than six orders of magnitude" precision shortfall. The two claims sit in the same sentence but the implied precision gap is understated by the "multiple degrees" framing — the actual dome prediction deviation is ~500 million degrees, which is nine orders of magnitude, not six. The text should align the order-of-magnitude claim with the actual computed ratio.

EXP-259 / WIN-016 c1 H1 [minor]: WIN-016 detail_evidence describes Gaia precision as "about a billionth of a degree" which rounds 2.78 × 10⁻⁹° down to 1 × 10⁻⁹° — an understatement by a factor of ~3. Acceptable order-of-magnitude English, but could read as misleading. Consider "about 3 billionths of a degree" or "roughly 2.8 × 10⁻⁹°" for precision.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H4 [minor]: "The BOU-2017 trilemma below holds whichever published rule the dome elects to defend" overstates scope. The trilemma's arithmetic (|−10.9|/3 ≈ 3.6×) is rule-specific to the ±3 nT amplitude rule; it is meaningless under the Tier 1 coherence rule. The structural collapse (accept or revise = concede either way) generalizes, but the trilemma as the reader encounters it below is amplitude-specific. Fix: soften to "The BOU-2017 trilemma below collapses to the same essential admission — accept the rule and concede, or revise it and admit the prediction was never discriminating — whichever rule the dome elects to defend." Optional polish.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H5 [minor]: "the very signature the predictions.html rule asks the August 2026 measurement to lack" is doubly negated (rule defines falsification as ABSENCE of signal; dome predicts PRESENCE; paragraph says the rule asks the measurement to LACK the signature). Casual reader will trip on parsing. Fix: re-word positively, e.g. "the very Chapman-pattern coherence the dome must observe in 2026 to avoid falsification was already present in BOU 2017." Optional polish.

PRED-106 post-closure rubric missing silent-SCRUB outcome: rubric covers (i) silent_ignore (PENDING badge remains), (ii) goalpost-move (window relabeled), (iii) dismiss-as-background (magnitude response). Missing fourth: silent SCRUB — PRED-106 quietly removed from predictions.html and count rebased without acknowledgement. issues.description: PRED-106 detail_reasoning understates Ca²⁺ magnitude mismatch. At Earth's ambient ~50 µT, Ca²⁺ cyclotron resonance is ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz. Dome's 16 Hz corresponds to ~21 µT (roughly a third to two-thirds of Earth's field depending on latitude). Should add MAGNITUDE NOTE when updating post-closure.

SEC-4.2.7a P2: "scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km" assumes proportional scaling of dome-published Moon altitude when h_Sun is set to alternative physical limit (4,200 km). Dome does NOT explicitly publish a scaled h_Moon for that limit. Assertion is an inference; mark as "if the dome applied proportional scaling" to avoid presenting an unstated dome claim as published.

SEC-4.2.7a P3 station-position probability argument uses IID uniform distribution as null model, but magnetic observatories are sited at specific geophysical locations (avoiding industrial interference, high-latitude auroral zones, etc.) — NOT uniform-random. The 10⁻²⁷ probability is correct for IID-uniform; it is irrelevant to the actual distribution of observatory siting decisions. The argument is still valid (the nine station positions are highly clustered relative to any defensible null) but needs a 1-sentence disclaimer.
issues.notes: From SEC-4.2.7a.c1 minor H2. The kill is unaffected; the framing needs a caveat.

issues.description: WIN-044 detail_evidence unit inconsistency: lead paragraph cites "r = 5,715 km (V50.6)" and "r = 9,983 km (V51)" with km units, but the technical-source paragraph restates these as plain numbers without units. issues.description: WIN-044 detail_evidence formula citation: transliterates dome formula as "ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF" but raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 21 publishes it explicitly as "delta_Z = baseline x coverage x FSF". The discrepancy (ΔB vs delta_Z) is minor but technically incorrect — the dome's formula uses Z-component notation. Fix: quote verbatim "delta_Z" and note this is the dome's published variable name.

WIN-044 detail_evidence: "purely geometric" overstates FSF independence from physical parameters. Under V51, H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619) with parameters chosen to fit dome's refraction model — FSF ratio depends on those physical-parameter choices. "Geometric in structure" or "derived from the dome's geometric model" is more accurate than "purely geometric."

PRED-106 post-closure rubric does not account for silent-scrub scenario (dome quietly removes prediction without acknowledgment). Add scrub detection: if PRED-106 is absent from predictions.html at first post-closure check, record as failure-by-deletion.

PRED-106 detail_reasoning references WIN-071 as dome-cited phantom. Curmudgeon recommends audit of all PRED entries in predictions.json where notes contains WIN-NNN references — verify they match actual wins.json entries. Mismatched WIN refs undermine credibility.

PRED-106 detail_reasoning understates the magnitude problem. At Earth ambient B (~50 µT), Ca²⁺ ICR would require ~5 MHz, not 16 Hz. Add a short MAGNITUDE NOTE quantifying the gap between published ICR frequency and the applied 16 Hz / 50 µT conditions.

WIN-026 c4: detail_verdict_text states monitor.py hardcodes both solar convergence sides as fact, but no specific verifiable artifact (commit SHA, file path, raw-text snippet) is cited. Verification provenance is missing.
WIN-026 c4: detail_verdict_text has latent rhetorical drag — "anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point is never tested by the script" phrasing could be clearer.

issues.description:
SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H0 [minor]: Text reads "the same Tier 1 block of predictions.html publishing the rule also publishes Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" — curmudgeon verified on 2026-04-30 that the Tier 1 coherence rule (

at line 125) and the 0.0 nT systematic quote (

at line 113) are in separate

blocks within the Tier 1 section, not the same block. Phrasing improved from prior "same line" to "same Tier 1 block" but may still be misleading if sub-blocks differ. Consider narrowing to "same Tier 1 section" or adding a parenthetical.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H1 [moderate]: The EXP-275 paragraph cites BOU-2017 single-station temporal coherence as preempting the dome Tier 1 coherence rule (which requires "coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5"). A sophisticated dome defender can retreat: BOU-2017 alone was not a ≥3-station r > 0.5 coherence test. The paragraph needs to explicitly invoke the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017, documenting ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern) as satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold — not BOU-2017 alone.

WIN-026.c5 H1 [minor]: Closing sentence of the inserted EXP-282 defense-neutralization paragraph reads "no aetheric-refraction caveat about the 5,733 km figure can manufacture parallel rays from a finite source" — this claim is asserted without argument. A one-sentence justification would strengthen it: any aetheric collimation mechanism capable of parallelizing radial-fan rays from a finite source must (a) specify a refractive-index profile, (b) leave observed apparent solar diameter (~0.53°) unchanged, and (c) satisfy other tight observational constraints simultaneously — the conjunction is either over-determined or introduces free parameters, making it unfalsifiable hand-waving.

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275 para: parenthetical "the same predictions.html line publishing the rule also publishes Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" is factually wrong — the rule and globe-prediction live on different lines/blocks (lines 113 vs 125). Fix to "same Tier 1 block" (3-word change).

SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275 para: BOU-2017 single-station evidence does not satisfy the dome rule requiring >=3 stations with r>0.5. The preempting evidence must either (a) cite additional stations or (b) reframe as "dome rule is self-defeating because its own Tier 1 requirement cannot be met by any single eclipse station."

SEC-4.2.7a headline paragraph: "inflating the Moon apparent diameter by the same factor" should read "physical diameter." Apparent/angular diameter is invariant under R_p ∝ h_Moon with totality preservation; only the physical disc-surface radius inflates. Minor phrasing change.

SEC-4.2.7a P1 robustness paragraph self-contradicting clause: "Moon altitude required to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49x the dome published 2,534 km." The 123,000 km / 49x figure corresponds to the FURTHEST station (not closest), since the closest station (Coimbra-San Pablo, Δr=75 km) is ALREADY inside the 85-km band. Fix: replace "closest min-distance" with "farthest station (maximum Δr)" and clarify the 49x applies to reaching all-stations coverage.

SEC-4.2.7a P1: Coimbra-San Pablo closest-pair Δr=75 km is stated as "just inside the 85-km band" but this is only true under the R_disc/1 convention. Under the stricter AE-projection convention cited in P1, this pair shifts to Δr=75→something else. The paragraph does not clarify that 75 km is the convention-specific figure — add the qualifier.

PRED-106 detail_reasoning identifies phantom WIN-071 citation but does not connect it to the broader dome pattern of internal-consistency drift. Add sentence noting this is part of a pattern (phantom citations, hardcoded values, post-hoc derivations) discussed in §2.x Self-Contradictions section.

WIN-030 detail_verdict_text post-hoc boilerplate: curmudgeon notes the sentence could specifically note that the dome curve-fitting derivation is itself a curve fit. Low-priority rhetorical sharpening deferred.

WIN-030 detail_evidence: nested-model triviality sentence (ellipse fit can never produce worse RMS than circle) is correct but unanchored to literature. Optional improvement: add AIC/BIC citation link (Burnham & Anderson, Model Selection and Multimodel Inference, §1.2–§2.5) via existing doi anchor. Deferred — the sentence re-uses the existing AIC/BIC citation context.

WIN-030 detail_evidence length has grown to ~2321c (+43% from c3 baseline). Optional: split first paragraph at WGS84-circularity boundary by inserting

after the nested-model triviality sentence. The TLDR_evidence also does not cover the WGS84 preemption argument added in c3. Low-priority cosmetic.
